lyrics:
Lines in the dust try to measure my stride
Stones in a row say which side is "my side."
Paper decrees, ink pretending to rule
Fear wears a badge, calling caution a tool

They tell me, "Stand here, you are safer in place."
But safety for some is erasure of grace
I'll honor the laws that defend what is frail
But not the cold cages that cherish the jail

Show me the boundary that sharpens my sight
Not the one that keeps me small
I follow the laws that protect the weak
And I walk through the rest of the wall
If justice is just obedience
Then justice is no law at all

I answer to consequence, not to control
To the weight of my footprint on body and soul
If my freedom crushes the breath of another
The law I must follow is: "See them as brother."

Show me the boundary that keeps all alive
Not the fence of someone's fear
I follow the laws that remember the lost
Not the ones that disappear
If order demands we vanish
I refuse to volunteer

A boundary drawn in kindness

Is a river I can keep

A boundary born of power

Is a vow I will not keep

The only law I bow to
Is the one that guards the weak
From the weight of my own freedom
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track opens with a maelstrom of technical guitar work, immediately establishing a dense, aggressive texture and a defiant, high-stakes emotional atmosphere. The energy is exceptionally high and unrelenting, driven by powerful, intricate drumming and layers of distorted guitars that create an urgent and assertive feel. The production is modern and polished, creating a massive sonic space where the clear, powerful vocals sit prominently atop the intense instrumentation without feeling cluttered. A defining quality is the dynamic contrast between the technically complex verses and the soaring, anthemic chorus, punctuated by a virtuosic and melodic guitar solo that showcases extreme musical skill.

review.review_text: PCBender comes out swinging on 'Law,' a 5-minute waltz-time anthem in B minor that shouldn't work at 129 BPM but absolutely does, the 3/4 groove giving the whole thing a rolling, unstoppable momentum rather than a march. The chorus lands the argument cleanly: 'I follow the laws that protect the weak / and I walk through the rest of the wall' is the kind of line that earns its anthemic treatment, and the production delivers, with vocals sitting right up front over layers of distorted guitar that push without burying the words. The guitar solo is the track's other peak, melodic enough to feel earned rather than decorative. It's the sort of principled, plainspoken rock that recalls prime-era John Hiatt or the more righteous end of early Tom Petty, music that knows what it believes and doesn't flinch.
anchors_used: lyric quote: 'I follow the laws that protect the weak / and I walk through the rest of the wall'
